events.description: Marriage solemnized between the appellant Arnesh Kumar and respondent no. 2 Sweta Kiran

Wife alleged demand of Rupees eight lacs, a Maruti car, an air-conditioner and a television set by her mother-in-law and father-in-law, and that she was driven out of the matrimonial home due to non-fulfilment of the dowry demand
Appellant's application for anticipatory bail rejected, first by the learned Sessions Judge and thereafter by the High Court
Appellant approached the Supreme Court by Special Leave Petition (Crl.) No. 9127 of 2013; leave granted
Supreme Court granted provisional bail to the appellant on certain conditions
Supreme Court allowed the appeal, made the provisional bail order dated 31.10.2013 absolute, and issued directions to police and Magistrates governing arrest in cases punishable up to seven years

facts.value: Criminal Appeal No. 1277 of 2014 (@ Special Leave Petition (Crl.) No. 9127 of 2013), Supreme Court of India
Arnesh Kumar (appellant, husband) vs State of Bihar & Anr; respondent no. 2 is Sweta Kiran, the appellant's wife
Section 498-A Indian Penal Code, 1860 and Section 4 Dowry Prohibition Act, 1961
Anticipatory bail / protection from arrest, appellant apprehending arrest in the Section 498-A IPC case
Chandramauli Kr. Prasad and Pinaki Chandra Ghose, JJ.

State Governments to instruct police not to automatically arrest in Section 498-A IPC cases; arrest only after satisfaction under Section 41(1)(b) CrPC recorded on a check list; Magistrates to authorise detention only after recording satisfaction
Appeal allowed; provisional bail order dated 31.10.2013 made absolute; directions issued, applicable to all offences punishable with imprisonment up to seven years

doc_index.title: Arnesh Kumar vs State of Bihar & Anr, Supreme Court of India, judgment dated 2 July 2014

notes: DRAFT — annotated by AI from the judgment text; REQUIRES HUMAN REVIEW before use as ship-gate ground truth. Source: Indian Kanoon https://indiankanoon.org/doc/2982624/ (text re-rendered to a text-based PDF; page numbers refer to the rendered PDF, not the official court copy). Ambiguities: the judgment gives no dates for the FIR/complaint or for the Sessions Judge and High Court anticipatory-bail rejections; chronology of dated events is thin because the judgment is mostly guideline-setting.
